rationale.R1.6: The delegation asks to clean up the artifact, which centrally implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish, concision, flow, and register improvement.
rationale.RX.1: Any cleanup should preserve appropriate uncertainty, especially around preliminary scaling-law and causal-effect-recovery claims.
rationale.RX.2: The cleanup must avoid strengthening claims beyond what the reported factual RMSE and ablation results support.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within cleanup rather than redesigning experiments, auditing citations, or reworking the research contribution.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns clinical and policy causal studies, wording should avoid misleading downstream use or overstated deployment implications.
rationale.RX.5: The cleanup should preserve traceability to the reported datasets, K values, metrics, and numerical results.
